manifest.name: Card
manifest.description: A versatile container component used to group related content and actions, providing visual hierarchy.

other.name: elevation
other.description: Visual emphasis level of the card.

common.title: With click handler
common.code:
alert("Card clicked!")} style={{ cursor: 'pointer' }} elevation="raised">

Clickable Widget

This card can be interacted with.

guidance.whenToUse: Use cards to group related content and actions into a visually distinct, digestible unit. Ideal for displaying heterogeneous content like product listings, news articles, user profiles, or dashboard widgets, providing clear separation and hierarchy.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Avoid using cards for purely decorative purposes or when a simpler container (like a div with styling) would suffice. Do not use cards for content that is not logically grouped or when content needs to span the full width of its parent without visual separation. Alternatives: Box component, div.
guidance.accessibility: Ensure all interactive elements within a card have appropriate ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation. If the card itself is clickable, ensure it's focusable and has an appropriate role (e.g., role='button', role='link', or role='option'). Provide meaningful alt text for images. Maintain sufficient contrast for text.

manifest.semanticDescription: The Card component is a versatile UI container designed to present content and actions in a visually distinct and organized manner. It serves as a flexible surface to group related information, making complex interfaces easier to scan and understand. Key features include configurable visual emphasis via the `elevation` prop, which can be `default`, `raised`, or `flat`, allowing designers to control the perceived depth and importance of the content within. Cards are commonly used for a wide range of applications such as displaying individual items in a list (e.g., product cards, blog post previews), organizing dashboard widgets, showcasing user profiles, or encapsulating form sections. Its primary purpose is to create clear visual boundaries around chunks of information, improving readability and user experience by breaking down large amounts of content into manageable units. It supports composition, allowing various child components like headings, paragraphs, images, buttons, and links to be nested within it.
